summary: Feature request to expose the private `canvasSize` field as a public `CanvasSize` property on both `SKCanvasView` and `SKGLView` in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, matching the pattern already used by every other SkiaSharp view platform.

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Both Blazor view files still have canvasSize as a private field with no public accessor. PR #1832 exposed Dpi but not CanvasSize.

analysis.summary: Both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor have a private `canvasSize` field but do not expose it as a public property. Every other SkiaSharp view platform (WinUI, WPF, WindowsForms, macOS, iOS, Android, Tizen, Uno, MAUI) already exposes `public SKSize CanvasSize`. PR #1832 exposed `Dpi` on the Blazor views but missed `CanvasSize`, creating a parity gap.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly an enhancement to existing views (not a new feature) — the private field exists and is used internally. All other platforms expose this property publicly. The reporter correctly references #1831 (same pattern for Dpi) and discussion #1888. A commenter also requests exposing the ElementReference for advanced JS interop, which is a related but separate request.

codeInvestigation.finding: Private field `canvasSize` (line 25) is used internally for size tracking and rendering. `Dpi` is already exposed as a public property (line 65), but CanvasSize has no public accessor.
Private field `canvasSize` (line 33) with same pattern — no public accessor. `Dpi` is exposed (line 72) but CanvasSize is not.
WinUI exposes `public SKSize CanvasSize { get; private set; }` — confirms the expected pattern.
MAUI exposes `public SKSize CanvasSize => lastCanvasSize;` — every other platform has this property public.

keySignals.text: analogous to #1831 & #1832 it may is useful to expose the canvasSize Property, too
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identifies the Dpi precedent and requests same treatment for CanvasSize.

keySignals.text: Add a public property on both components to expose the private field.
keySignals.interpretation: Clear, actionable request — knows the fix is trivial.

keySignals.text: anyone who wants to do non-trivial JS interop needs the htmlCanvas ElementReference exposed
keySignals.interpretation: Related but separate request — exposing ElementReference for JS interop is a different enhancement.

resolution.hypothesis: The CanvasSize property was simply overlooked when the Blazor views were created. PR #1832 added Dpi but not CanvasSize. A one-line property addition to each view class resolves the gap.

proposals.title: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
proposals.description: Add `public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize;` to both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs, matching the pattern used by all other SkiaSharp view platforms.

proposals.title: Access canvasSize via SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs
proposals.description: As a workaround, the canvas size can be obtained from the SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs.Info.Size property inside the OnPaintSurface callback, though this only works during paint and requires scaling awareness.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial fix that achieves full platform parity. The private field already exists and is maintained — only a public accessor is needed.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with clear scope and trivial implementation. All other platforms expose this property. Needs a PR to add the public accessor.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the request and confirm the gap with a proposed fix
actions.comment:
Thanks for reporting this. You're correct — both `SKCanvasView` and `SKGLView` in `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` have `canvasSize` as a private field, while every other SkiaSharp view platform exposes it as `public SKSize CanvasSize`. This was likely an oversight when PR #1832 added the `Dpi` property.

The fix would be adding a single line to each view:

```csharp
public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize;
```

In the meantime, you can access the canvas size from within the `OnPaintSurface` callback via `e.Info.Size` (scaled) or calculate it from the surface dimensions.

actions.description: Cross-reference the Dpi exposure issue that established the pattern
